text:
Once upon a time in a small town nestled among forests, there lived a peculiar fox who possessed magical ears. Every night, just before moonlight kissed the trees, she'd hear the whispering winds tell stories. One night, while tuning in to a particularly enchanting tale about a phoenix resurrection dance, she caught a tiny mouse gnawing on a dropped crumb.

"The mouse spoke," the mouse continued with excitement. "It said you could hear mice like that everywhere. I live near the old oak tree. Once, just three months ago, I saw a phoenix fluttering past the moon. They said it was only a young one, too young to have flown that far."

The fox paused, considering the mouse mouse. "That's quite remarkable. Hmmmm..."

She pondered for a moment. "Well," she thought. "If a phoenix flies that far... I wouldn't take mice. But perhaps... I could return that gift. Maybe one time?"

The tiny mouse twitch
